cards.title: OpenAI Expands ChatGPT for Families
cards.body: OpenAI is hiring a dedicated product manager to build ChatGPT experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults. The move signals the company's push to deepen household integration.

cards.title: Meta Removes Controversial Instagram AI Feature
cards.body: Meta pulled a feature allowing AI to reference public Instagram content after user backlash. The company acknowledged the creative tool missed the mark.

cards.title: Apple Sues OpenAI for Trade Secrets
cards.body: Apple filed a lawsuit alleging OpenAI stole trade secrets, with allegations directed at senior leadership including a former employee. The suit marks escalating legal tensions in the AI sector.

cards.title: Open Source AI Dominance Grows
cards.body: Hugging Face CEO reports open source AI is booming, with the platform now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. The company has become a GitHub-like hub for AI models and datasets.

cards.title: SK Hynix Raises Record $26.5B
cards.body: SK Hynix completed the biggest foreign IPO in US history, raising $26.5 billion amid AI chip demand. Policymakers are pushing the company to build new US fabrication facilities.

cards.title: GPT-5.6 Powers Microsoft Copilot 365
cards.body: OpenAI announced GPT-5.6 will remain the preferred model for Microsoft Copilot 365 productivity suite. The partnership continues despite prior speculation about a potential breakup.

cards.title: OpenAI's No. 2 Executive Steps Down
cards.body: Fidji Simo departed her role as OpenAI's second-highest executive after extended medical leave. The departure creates a leadership gap amid IPO planning and competition with Anthropic.

cards.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6 Model Family
cards.body: OpenAI released a new family of models with GPT-5.6, offering improvements in cybersecurity and other areas. The launch strengthens its competitive position in the market.

cards.title: AI Agent Startup Raises $100M Autonomously
cards.body: Lyzr used its own AI agent to lead a $100 million fundraising round. The demonstration proved the effectiveness of its enterprise AI agent product.

cards.title: OpenAI Shutters AI Browser Atlas
cards.body: OpenAI is discontinuing its Atlas AI browser after less than a year of operation. Agentic browsing features will migrate to the desktop app and Chrome extension.

cards.title: Musk Reassures Anthropic on Hosting
cards.body: Elon Musk praised Anthropic's AI initiatives and promised not to cut off model hosting. With $40 billion in revenue at stake, trust in infrastructure becomes critical.

cards.title: The $3 Trillion AI ROI Question
cards.body: The AI return on investment debate has intensified with even larger financial stakes involved. The discussion carries significant consequences for enterprise adoption decisions.

cards.title: Meta Launches Muse Spark 1.1
cards.body: Meta entered competitive AI coding with Muse Spark 1.1, targeting large agentic workloads and enterprise migrations. The tool emphasizes bug fixes and automated code refactoring.

cards.title: New York Times Alleges OpenAI Hidden Evidence
cards.body: News publishers claim OpenAI concealed tools identifying copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs. The motion for sanctions escalates an ongoing copyright infringement lawsuit.

cards.title: Google Mandates AI Disclosure in Ads
cards.body: Google will now require advertisers to disclose when AI creates synthetic or altered content in ads. The policy expands beyond election ads to all synthetic advertising.

cards.title: Gradium Raises $100M for AI Voice
cards.body: Paris-based voice AI startup Gradium raised $100 million in seed funding backed by Nvidia. The company plans Bay Area expansion to compete for top AI talent.

cards.title: Government Safety Review of Frontier Models
cards.body: Details remain unclear on how government bodies approved OpenAI's frontier model releases. The oversight process between regulators and AI companies lacks public transparency.

cards.title: Stop Meta AI from Using Your Photos
cards.body: Meta's Muse Image generates AI pictures using photos from public Instagram accounts. Users can opt out by adjusting account privacy settings.

cards.title: Meta's New AI Chips Begin Production
cards.body: Meta's custom AI chips will enter production in September using modular architecture. The design allows rapid iteration as AI hardware needs evolve.

cards.title: OpenAI Targets Families with ChatGPT
cards.body: OpenAI is hiring a dedicated product manager to build ChatGPT experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults. The move signals expansion beyond traditional enterprise and consumer markets.

cards.title: Meta Removes Controversial AI Feature
cards.body: Meta has removed an AI feature on Instagram that generated images using public user photos after widespread backlash. The company acknowledged the feature missed the mark despite its original intent to empower creators.

cards.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Trade Secrets
cards.body: Apple alleges OpenAI misappropriated trade secrets, with misconduct allegedly directed by senior leadership including a former employee. The lawsuit represents a major legal escalation in AI industry tensions.

cards.title: Open Source AI Boom Accelerates
cards.body: Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue reports open source AI is booming, with the platform now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. The company has become the de facto GitHub for AI model sharing.

cards.title: GPT 5.6 Powers Microsoft Copilot 365
cards.body: OpenAI's new GPT 5.6 model family will continue powering Microsoft's workplace productivity suite Copilot 365. The partnership deepens despite ongoing speculation about potential separation.

cards.title: OpenAI's No. 2 Executive Steps Down
cards.body: Fidji Simo is leaving her role as OpenAI's No. 2 after extended medical leave, creating a leadership gap at a critical time. The departure comes as OpenAI eyes an IPO and competes with Anthropic.

cards.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6 Model Family
cards.body: OpenAI released its new GPT-5.6 family of models with improvements in cybersecurity and other key areas. The release marks the company's latest competitive push in frontier AI.

cards.title: AI Agent Raises $100M Fundraise Itself
cards.body: Lyzr, an enterprise AI agent startup, let its own AI agent conduct a $100 million fundraising round. The demonstration serves as proof-of-concept for the startup's technology capabilities.

cards.title: OpenAI Shuts Down AI Browser Atlas
cards.body: OpenAI is sunsetting its Atlas AI-powered browser after less than a year, but moving some agentic features to its desktop app and Chrome extension. The shift reflects a pivot in product strategy.

cards.title: Meta Launches Muse Spark AI Coding Tool
cards.body: Meta entered the competitive AI coding space with Muse Spark 1.1, designed to handle large agentic workloads and bug fixes. The tool targets enterprises seeking code migration automation.

cards.title: Times Alleges OpenAI Hid Copyright Evidence
cards.body: The New York Times claims OpenAI concealed tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs. The accusation escalates the ongoing copyright infringement lawsuit with new sanctions motions.

cards.title: Google Mandates AI Disclosure in Ads
cards.body: Google now requires disclosure of AI-generated or synthetic content in ads, extending a previous election-ad-only requirement. The policy aims to increase transparency around AI-created advertising materials.

cards.title: Nvidia-Backed Gradium Raises $100M Seed
cards.body: Paris-based AI voice startup Gradium secured $100 million in seed funding backed by Nvidia to expand to Silicon Valley. The company aims to strengthen its position in the global AI ecosystem.

cards.title: Meta's AI Chips Enter Production September
cards.body: Meta's custom AI chips will begin production in September using a modular design approach. The company anticipates its AI needs will evolve rapidly before chips ship.

cards.title: Anthropic Reveals Claude's Inner Workings
cards.body: Anthropic developed a technique revealing what happens inside Claude as it answers questions and performs tasks. The research offers unprecedented insight into large language model reasoning processes.

cards.title: AI Architecture Foundations for IT Leaders
cards.body: As organizations expand AI use cases and move toward agentic systems, IT leaders face pressure to identify investments that remain valuable long-term. Rapid AI capability growth introduces both opportunity and risk.
